document.docAuthor: Mongkolsamrit, Suchada, Noisripoom, Wasana, Tasanathai, Kanoksri, Kobmoo, Noppol, Thanakitpipattana, Donnaya, Khonsanit, Artit, Petcharad, Booppa, Sakolrak, Baramee & Himaman, Winanda
document.docTitle: Hevansia minuta Tasanathai, Noisripoom & Mongkolsamrit 2022, sp. nov.
document.masterDocTitle: Comprehensive treatise of Hevansia and three new genera Jenniferia, Parahevansia and Polystromomyces on spiders in Cordycipitaceae from Thailand

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Comprehensive treatise of Hevansia and three new genera Jenniferia, Parahevansia and Polystromomyces on spiders in Cordycipitaceae from Thailand

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Plant Microbe Interaction Research Team, National Center for Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology (BIOTEC), 113 Thailand Science Park, Phahonyothin Road, Khlong Nueng, Khlong Luang, Pathum Thani, 12120, Thailand

figureCitation.captionText: Figure 4. Hevansia minuta a fungus on a spider (BBH 30490) b perithecia c ascus d ascus tip e filiform, whole ascospore f, g phialides with conidia h, i colonies on OA at 21 days (h obverse, i reverse) j, k colonies on PDA at 21 days (j obverse, k reverse). Scale bars: 5 mm (a), 100 µm (b), 50 µm (c), 10 µm (d, f, g), 20 µm (e).

subSubSection.paragraph:
Thailand, Chumphon Province, Heo Lom Waterfall, 9°43'45.04"N , 98°40'52.71"E , on spider (Web builder, Araneae , Theridiidae ,
Meotipa
sp.) attached to the underside of a dicot leaf of forest plants, 30 May 2011, K. Tasanathai, P. Srikitikulchai, A. Khonsanit, K. Sansatchanon, D. Thanakitpipattana, MY6537.01 (BBH 30490, holotype), ex-type culture BCC 47519 isolated from ascospores.

caption.paragraph:
Figure 4.
Hevansia minuta
a fungus on a spider (BBH 30490) b perithecia c ascus d ascus tip e filiform, whole ascospore f, g phialides with conidia h, i colonies on OA at 21 days ( h obverse, i reverse) j, k colonies on PDA at 21 days ( j obverse, k reverse). Scale bars: 5 mm ( a ), 100 µm ( b ), 50 µm ( c ), 10 µm ( d, f, g ), 20 µm ( e ).

subSubSection.paragraph: Refers to the small stroma of this species.

Spider host covered by white mycelium. Sexual morph: Stromata stipitate, arising from the dorsal region of the host, solitary, cylindrical to enlarging apically, white to cream, 10 mm long, 1 mm broad. Fertile head oval, ca. 2-2.5 mm long, ca. 1.5 mm broad. Perithecia completely immersed, narrowly ovoid, 400-500 x 100-170 µm . Asci cylindrical, 8-spored, 325-450 x 3-5 µm , with cap 2-5 µm thick. Ascospores hyaline, filiform, whole ascospores, 320-450 x 0.5-1.5 µm . Asexual morph: Conidiogenous cells phialidic scattered along with the stipe. Phialides solitary, globose to ovoid, arising from the mycelium, 5-7 x 5-6 µm , distinct necks, 1-2 x 0.5-1 µm . Conidia hyaline, fusiform, 2-7 x 2-3 µm .
Colonies on OA attaining a diam. of 15-18(20) mm in 21 days, cottony with high mycelium density, white. Conidia and reproductive structures not observed.
Colonies on PDA attaining a diam. of 8-9(10) mm in 21 days, cottony with high mycelium density, white, reverse pale yellow (161C-D). Conidia and reproductive structures not observed.
Specimens were found on the underside of dicot leaves of forest plants.

Thailand , Chumphon Province , Heo Lom Waterfall , 9°43'45.04"N , 98°40'52.71"E , on spider (Web builder, Araneae , Theridiidae ,
Meotipa
sp.) attached to the underside of a dicot leaf of forest plants, 30 May 2011 , K. Tasanathai , P. Srikitikulchai , A. Khonsanit , K. Sansatchanon , D. Thanakitpipattana , MY06537.02 ( BBH 30490 , paratype ), ex-paratype culture BCC 47520 isolated from conidia
.

Hevansia minuta
differs significantly from
H. novoguineensis
and
H. nelumboides
in the shape of the fertile heads, which is oval in
H. minuta
and disc-shaped, slightly convex on the upper surface in
H. novoguineensis
and
H. nelumboides
. Additionally,
H. minuta
differs from
H. novoguineensis
in the size of the perithecia. In
H. minuta
, perithecia are smaller than those reported for
H. novoguineensis
(400-500 x 100-170 µm vs. 500-750 x 200-300 µm ) (Table 2 ). Synnema in
H. minuta
was not observed in the natural specimen, while the other species in
Hevansia
produce synnemata (Table 3 ).
Hevansia minuta
does not produce pigment in culture. Meanwhile,
H. novoguineensis
produces a purplish-red pigment diffusing in PDA plates.

caption.paragraph:
Table 2. Morphological comparisons of sexual morphs in
Hevansia
,
Jenniferia
and
Polystromomyces
.

tr.td: Stipitate, solitary, white to cream, 10 mm long, 1 mm broad
Oval, ca. 2-2.5 mm long, ca. 1.5 mm broad
Stipitate, white, 4 mm long, 0.4 mm broad

bibRefCitation.author: Kobayasi, Y
bibRefCitation.refString: Kobayasi, Y, Shimizu, D, 1977. Some species of Cordyceps and its allies on spiders. Kew Bulletin 31 (3): 557 - 566, DOI: https://doi.org/10.2307/4119402
bibRefCitation.title: Some species of Cordyceps and its allies on spiders.

tr.td: Stipitate, solitary, or multiple, cylindrical, white to pale yellow, 3-5 mm long, 0.5-1 mm broad
Disc-shaped, upper surface slightly convex, 1-3 x 1-2 mm

Spider ( Thomisidae , Diaea cf. dorsata ,
Diaea
sp.)
Perithecia aggregated in clusters forming a cushion
Whole ascospores with septate part-spores alternately connected with thread-like structures, up to 400 µm long, each cell narrowly fusiform, 10-15 x 1-2 µm , filiform regions, 35-45 x 0.2-0.8 µm
Perithecia aggregated in clusters forming a cushion
Whole ascospores with septate part-spores alternately connected with thread-like structures, up to 680 µm long, each cell narrowly fusiform, 10-20 x 1-2 µm , filiform regions, 30-50 x 0.2-0.8 µm
Stipitate, multiple, moderate yellow, 8-12 mm long, 1-3 mm broad
Disc-shaped, upper surface slightly convex, 3-4 x 2-3.5 mm

caption.paragraph:
Table 3. Morphological comparisons of asexual morphs in
Hevansia
,
Jenniferia
and
Parahevansia
.

tr.td: Simple, solitary (rarely two or three together), cylindrical, cream, up to 6 mm long, 45-100 µm broad
Globose, 3-4.5 µm broad, with distinct necks, 1-2 x 0.5 µm

bibRefCitation.author: Hywel-Jones, NL
bibRefCitation.refString: Hywel-Jones, NL, 1996. Akanthomyces on spiders in Thailand. Mycological Research 100 (9): 1065 - 1070, DOI: https://doi.org/10.1016/S0953-7562(96)80214-0
bibRefCitation.title: Akanthomyces on spiders in Thailand.

bibRefCitation.author: Huang, B
bibRefCitation.refString: Huang, B, Wang, S, Fan, M, Li, Z, 2000. Two species of Akanthomyces from Dabieshan mountains. Junwu Xuebao 19: 172 - 174
bibRefCitation.title: Two species of Akanthomyces from Dabieshan mountains.

tr.td: Globose to ovoid, 5-7 x 5-6 µm with distinct necks, 1-2 x 0.5 µm

bibRefCitation.author: Kobayasi, Y
bibRefCitation.refString: Kobayasi, Y, Shimizu, D, 1977. Some species of Cordyceps and its allies on spiders. Kew Bulletin 31 (3): 557 - 566, DOI: https://doi.org/10.2307/4119402
bibRefCitation.title: Some species of Cordyceps and its allies on spiders.

tr.td: Multiple, cylindrical, occasionally acuminate apex, white, up to 8 mm long, 50-200 µm broad
Globose to subglobose, 4-6 x 4-6 µm , with distinct necks, 0.5-1.5 x 0.5-1 µm
Multiple, slender, acuminate apex, white to pale yellow, 3.5 mm long, 50-150 µm broad
Globose to ovoid, 5-6.5 x 4-6 µm broad, with distinct necks, 2-3 x 0.8-1.5 µm
Cylindrical, curved or slightly fusiform, 10.5-17.5 x 1.5-3 µm

bibRefCitation.author: Samson, RA
bibRefCitation.journalOrPublisher: Transactions of the British Mycological Society
bibRefCitation.refString: Samson, RA, Brady, BL, 1982. Akanthomyces novoguineensis sp. nov. Transactions of the British Mycological Society 79 (3): 571 - 572, DOI: https://doi.org/10.1016/S0007-1536(82)80065-X
bibRefCitation.title: Akanthomyces novoguineensis sp. nov.

tr.td: Multiple, simple, or branch, white to greyish-orange, 2.2-9 mm long, 112-520 µm broad
Globose to subglobose, cylindrical, or ellipsoid, 6-8.7 x 4-6.4 µm , with distinct necks, 1.4-3.2 x 0.8-1.8 µm
Ellipsoid, obovate to oblong, 6-10.3 x 2.4-4.4 µm

bibRefCitation.author: Hsieh, L
bibRefCitation.refString: Hsieh, L, Tzean, S, Wu, WJ, 1997. The Genus Akanthomyces on Spiders from Taiwan. Mycologia 89 (2): 319 - 324, DOI: https://doi.org/10.1080/00275514.1997.12026788
bibRefCitation.title: The Genus Akanthomyces on Spiders from Taiwan.

tr.td: Simple, cylindrical, cream-white, up to 12 mm long, 50-70 µm broad
Ellipsoid, 4.5-8.5 x 2-3.5 µm , with distinct necks, 1.5-3 x 0.5 µm

bibRefCitation.author: Hywel-Jones, NL
bibRefCitation.refString: Hywel-Jones, NL, 1996. Akanthomyces on spiders in Thailand. Mycological Research 100 (9): 1065 - 1070, DOI: https://doi.org/10.1016/S0953-7562(96)80214-0
bibRefCitation.title: Akanthomyces on spiders in Thailand.

taxonomicName.baseAuthorityName: Mongkolsamrit & Noisripoom & Tasanathai & Kobmoo & Thanakitpipattana & Khonsanit & Petcharad & Sakolrak & Himaman

tr.td: Multiple, clavate, grey, up to 3 mm long, 60-70 µm broad
Cylindrical, 3.5-6.5 x 1.5-2 µm , with distinct necks, 2-2.5 x 0.5 µm

bibRefCitation.author: Hywel-Jones, NL
bibRefCitation.refString: Hywel-Jones, NL, 1996. Akanthomyces on spiders in Thailand. Mycological Research 100 (9): 1065 - 1070, DOI: https://doi.org/10.1016/S0953-7562(96)80214-0
bibRefCitation.title: Akanthomyces on spiders in Thailand.

tr.td:
Spider ( Thomisidae , Diaea cf. dorsata ,
Diaea
sp.)

Two types of synnemata, long synnemata, cylindrical with blunt end, grey to pale brown, 2.5-5 mm long, 100-150 µm broad, middle of long synnemata, 50-80 μm broad; short synnemata, cylindrical, pale grey to dark grey, up to 450 µm long, 20-50 µm broad
Flask-shaped, 5-10 x 3-5 µm , with distinct necks, 2-3.5 x 0.5-1 µm
Multiple, cylindrical to clavate, greyish-brown, up to 800 µm long, 30-100 µm broad
Cylindrical, 7-16 x 2-5 µm , with distinct necks, 1-5 x 1-1.5 µm
Multiple, simple, brown at the sterile base, becoming grey white, up to 6 mm long, 50 µm broad
Obovoid to ellipsoid, 4-5.5 x 3-3.5 µm , with distinct necks, 2.5-3 x 0.5-1 µm

bibRefCitation.author: Hywel-Jones, NL
bibRefCitation.refString: Hywel-Jones, NL, 1996. Akanthomyces on spiders in Thailand. Mycological Research 100 (9): 1065 - 1070, DOI: https://doi.org/10.1016/S0953-7562(96)80214-0
bibRefCitation.title: Akanthomyces on spiders in Thailand.
